free_text: Measurement of activation cross sections of deuteron
induced reactions on natIr in the 17-50MeV energy range
High purity natural Ir (10.54 and 45.57 um)
The irradiated stack
contained 20 blocks of Ir-Al-Ta-Al foils.
Irradiation were done in a Faraday-cup like
target holder with beam intensity of I=100nA for 1.0 h.
No chemical separation was applied.
Five series of spectra were recorded.
Decay data were taken from NUDAT and LUND/BNLL online
data libraries.
Correction for attenuation of low energy gamma photons
in the Ir target foils was done.
The uncertainty on the median energy in the
foils includes the cumulative effects of uncertainty of
incident beam energy, uncertainty of stopping power of
each foil due to thickness variation and straggling
effects.
The uncertainty was calculated as the square
root of the quadratic summation of the independently
contributing parameters. Uncertainty of time parameters
was not included.
average beam intensity (7%),
target thickness and homogeneity (5%),
detector efficiency (5%)
statistical and peak fitting (1-20%)
The experimental data were compared to the
results of the theoretical model codes ALICE-D,
EMPIRE-D and TALYS (TENDL-2017)
The measured experimental data were compared to
those found in the literature.
Fitted excitation functions were used for
determination of integral physical yields.

31.34 MeV were deleted in subentry 015.
